items.headline: NATO member closes airspace to allied military over Middle East operations
items.body: Spain barred US aircraft involved in Iran operations from its airspace, extending an earlier ban on American use of joint military bases. The move tests alliance cohesion as Washington pledged to defend the NATO member while Madrid calls the war illegal.

items.headline: Australian states split on free transport as fuel shortages spread from strait closure
items.body: Victoria and Tasmania waived public transport fares through mid-year as Iran's grip on Hormuz drives energy costs higher. Federal government halved fuel excise while some stations report empty tanks despite stable national reserves.

items.headline: Africa and Latin America advance trade cooperation to bypass traditional partners
items.body: Trade ministers at WTO meeting proposed regular summits between regions representing nearly 90 countries and over two billion people. Initiative aims to boost South-South commerce currently at just 0.3 percent of global trade.

items.headline: Washington partially lifts four-month asylum freeze while maintaining ban for 40 countries
items.body: Homeland Security resumed processing applications from most countries after November pause affected four million cases. Ban remains for travel-ban nations including Cuba, Venezuela, Haiti, Afghanistan, and Iran.

meanwhile.text: SXSW celebrated 40th edition with 4,400 musicians from 50 countries performing across 300 showcases
Scientists created first lab-grown oesophagus that restored swallowing function in growing animal without immunosuppression
UK trials begin for blood test detecting 50 cancer types before symptoms appear using DNA fragments
meanwhile.search: multi-cancer early detection blood test UK 2026

meanwhile.text: Conservative Christian revival among US Gen Z shows better-educated adherents attending church more than non-believers
meanwhile.search: Gen Z Christian revival conservative faith attendance 2026

es.headline: Government and Catholic bishops finalize clergy abuse compensation system
es.body: Spain signed an agreement giving state oversight to Church reparations for time-barred sexual abuse cases. The framework takes effect mid-April with the ombudsman having final say over disputed compensation amounts.

es.headline: Spain summons Israeli envoy after Catholic leaders barred from Jerusalem church
es.body: Foreign minister protested Israeli police blocking the Latin Patriarch from Palm Sunday mass at Holy Sepulchre. Spain called the incident unacceptable and demanded normal worship be allowed at Christian holy sites.

no.headline: Rocket launch from Europe's first spaceport aborted seconds before ignition after fishing boat delays
no.body: A fishing vessel in the safety zone delayed the countdown at Andøya Spaceport, causing propellant temperatures to rise and forcing an abort. No new launch date has been announced for the satellite mission.

no.headline: Norway and Belgium sign cross-border CO2 transport deal to enable European carbon storage
no.body: Norway and Belgium signed an agreement to transport CO2 collected in Zeebrugge to storage sites beneath the North Sea. Under the proposal, a pipeline could be operational by 2031.

nl.headline: Healthcare providers fined for recruiting Indonesian nurses under false internships
nl.body: Authorities imposed nearly €700,000 in penalties on institutions that brought at least 62 Indonesian nursing graduates as students but used them as unpaid workers. The scheme violated employment laws by requiring work permits the employers never obtained.

nl.headline: Advisory council backs terrorism glorification ban but warns on rights safeguards
nl.body: Council of State endorsed proposed law criminalizing praise of terrorist attacks with up to three years prison. Body warned fundamental rights could be violated without proper application of European human rights rulings.
